name: spillorama-system

scripts.build: npm -w @spillorama/shared-types run build && npm -w @spillorama/game-client run build && npm -w @spillorama/admin-web run build && npm --prefix apps/backend run build
scripts.build:games: npm -w @spillorama/shared-types run build && npm -w @spillorama/game-client run build
scripts.build:visual-harness: npm -w @spillorama/shared-types run build && npm -w @spillorama/game-client run build:visual-harness
scripts.check:all: npm run check && npm run build && npm run test:compliance
scripts.lint:blink: npm run lint:css && npm run lint:no-backdrop-js
scripts.lint:all: npm run lint:css && npm run lint:no-backdrop-js && npm run lint:no-unsafe-html
scripts.perf:check:pilot-hw: npm run perf:collect:pilot-hw && npm run perf:compare:pilot-hw

//overrides_rationale: SEC-P0-003 (Bølge 2A 2026-04-28): targeted security overrides — these are NPM `overrides` entries that force vulnerable transitive deps to patched versions. We use overrides INSTEAD of `npm audit fix` because audit-fix's lockfile re-resolution bumps unrelated dev-deps (notably artillery's plugin chain) into newer @opentelemetry/* + protobufjs that have their own active CVEs. Each override is intentionally scoped to a specific CVE chain. (1) @xmldom/xmldom: pulled via pixi.js → 4 HIGH advisories (GHSA-2v35-w6hq-6mfw DoS + GHSA-f6ww-3ggp-fr8h XML-injection + GHSA-x6wf-f3px-wcqx + GHSA-j759-j44w-7fr8). (2) protobufjs ^8.0.3: GHSA-xq3m-2v4x-88gg arbitrary code exec in 8.0.0; only relevant via the same artillery+otlp chain that audit-fix wants to bump. Pinning to 8.0.3+ patches the CVE without forcing the whole otlp chain. Remove these overrides when upstream pixi.js / artillery resolve transitively. See FIN-P0-03 in docs/audit/SECURITY_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md.
